authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

reaches.name: Walnut Creek upstream of the confluence with Grayson Creek
reaches.fromDesc: Confluence with Grayson Creek near Pacheco/Martinez
reaches.toDesc: Upstream through the city of Walnut Creek and its headwater tributaries

reaches.name: Walnut Creek downstream of the confluence with Grayson Creek
reaches.fromDesc: Confluence with Grayson Creek near Pacheco/Martinez
reaches.toDesc: Downstream to Suisun Bay/Carquinez Strait near Martinez

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40(b)(121) — Walnut Creek (Contra Costa Co.)
14 CCR §7.40 — Hatchery Trout/Steelhead Footnote (**) Definition
sources.quotedText: Hatchery trout or steelhead in anadromous waters are those showing a healed adipose fin clip (adipose fin is absent). Unless otherwise provided, all other trout and steelhead must be immediately released.

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Streams layer record for gaz_id 237199, "Walnut Creek", Stream, Contra Costa Co., CA
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":237199,"gaz_name":"Walnut Creek","gaz_featureclass":"Stream","fcode":46000,"state_alpha":"CA","county_name":"Contra Costa"} — geometry multipoint; first point (Web Mercator -13588798.5933, 4582829.8472) converts to lon -122.070255, lat 38.024093, the creek's mouth near its confluence with Pacheco Creek in Martinez (matches Wikipedia infobox mouth coordinate 38°1'27"N 122°4'13"W), replacing the file's previous coordinate (-122.0653, 37.9101), which was off by ~12.7 km and instead sat near the creek's headwaters/source area in downtown Walnut Creek (Wikipedia infobox source: confluence of Las Trampas Creek and Tice Creek, 37°53'38"N 122°3'35"W).

regulations.humanSummary: Walnut Creek upstream of the Grayson Creek confluence open fourth Saturday in May through March 31.
regulations.verbatimText: Fourth Sat. in May through Mar. 31.

parameters.note: Applies whenever this reach is open (fourth Saturday in May through March 31).

regulations.humanSummary: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used.
regulations.verbatimText: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used.

parameters.counting_rule: Combined limit of hatchery trout and/or hatchery steelhead (fish showing a healed adipose fin clip); 2 fish daily, 4 fish in possession.

regulations.humanSummary: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead daily, 4 in possession.
regulations.verbatimText: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

regulations.humanSummary: Walnut Creek downstream of the Grayson Creek confluence is open all year.

parameters.counting_rule: Combined limit of hatchery trout and/or hatchery steelhead (fish showing a healed adipose fin clip); 2 fish daily, 4 fish in possession.

regulations.humanSummary: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead daily, 4 in possession.
regulations.verbatimText: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
